content.text: In Korean phonetics, the rule of {{c1::Vowel Length}} is defined as: Historically phonemic, but largely lost in modern Seoul dialect.
content.explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for Korean.
content.spanish: En la fonética de Korean, la regla de 'Vowel Length' es: Historically phonemic, but largely lost in modern Seoul dialect.
content.usage:
Rule: Vowel Length
Description: Historically phonemic, but largely lost in modern Seoul dialect.

content.text: In Korean phonetics, the rule of {{c1::Diphthongs}} is defined as: Korean has many y-glides (야, 여, 요, 유, 얘, 예) and w-glides (와, 워, 왜, 웨).
content.explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for Korean.
content.spanish: En la fonética de Korean, la regla de 'Diphthongs' es: Korean has many y-glides (야, 여, 요, 유, 얘, 예) and w-glides (와, 워, 왜, 웨).
content.usage:
Rule: Diphthongs
Description: Korean has many y-glides (야, 여, 요, 유, 얘, 예) and w-glides (와, 워, 왜, 웨).

content.text: In Korean phonetics, the rule of {{c1::Vowel Harmony}} is defined as: While less strict than in the past, it still influences onomatopoeia and some verb endings.
content.explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for Korean.
content.spanish: En la fonética de Korean, la regla de 'Vowel Harmony' es: While less strict than in the past, it still influences onomatopoeia and some verb endings.
content.usage:
Rule: Vowel Harmony
Description: While less strict than in the past, it still influences onomatopoeia and some verb endings.

content.text: In Korean phonetics, the rule of {{c1::Plain (Laxis)}} is defined as: Slightly aspirated, voiced between vowels.
content.explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for Korean.
content.spanish: En la fonética de Korean, la regla de 'Plain (Laxis)' es: Slightly aspirated, voiced between vowels.
content.usage:
Rule: Plain (Laxis)
Description: Slightly aspirated, voiced between vowels.

content.text: In Korean phonetics, the rule of {{c1::Aspirated}} is defined as: Strongly aspirated.
content.explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for Korean.
content.spanish: En la fonética de Korean, la regla de 'Aspirated' es: Strongly aspirated.
content.usage:
Rule: Aspirated
Description: Strongly aspirated.

content.text: In Korean phonetics, the rule of {{c1::Tense (Fortis)}} is defined as: Produced with a "stiff" voice, never aspirated.
content.explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for Korean.
content.spanish: En la fonética de Korean, la regla de 'Tense (Fortis)' es: Produced with a "stiff" voice, never aspirated.
content.usage:
Rule: Tense (Fortis)
Description: Produced with a "stiff" voice, never aspirated.

content.text: In Korean phonetics, the rule of {{c1::The Liquid /l/ (ㄹ)}} is defined as: Pronounced as a flap [ɾ] between vowels and a lateral [l] at the end of a syllable.
content.explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for Korean.
content.spanish: En la fonética de Korean, la regla de 'The Liquid /l/ (ㄹ)' es: Pronounced as a flap [ɾ] between vowels and a lateral [l] at the end of a syllable.
content.usage:
Rule: The Liquid /l/ (ㄹ)
Description: Pronounced as a flap [ɾ] between vowels and a lateral [l] at the end of a syllable.

content.text: In Korean phonetics, the rule of {{c1::Batchim (Syllable Finals)}} is defined as: Only 7 consonant sounds can be heard at the end of a syllable ([p, t, k, m, n, ŋ, l]). All other consonants are neutralized (e.g., 's' ㅅ, 't' ㅌ, 'ch' ㅊ, 'd' ㄷ all sound like [t] at the end of a block, e.g., *kkoch* 꽃 -> [kkot]).
content.explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for Korean.
content.spanish: En la fonética de Korean, la regla de 'Batchim (Syllable Finals)' es: Only 7 consonant sounds can be heard at the end of a syllable ([p, t, k, m, n, ŋ, l]). All other consonants are neutralized (e.g., 's' ㅅ, 't' ㅌ, 'ch' ㅊ, 'd' ㄷ all sound like [t] at the end of a block, e.g., *kkoch* 꽃 -> [kkot]).
content.usage:
Rule: Batchim (Syllable Finals)
Description: Only 7 consonant sounds can be heard at the end of a syllable ([p, t, k, m, n, ŋ, l]). All other consonants are neutralized (e.g., 's' ㅅ, 't' ㅌ, 'ch' ㅊ, 'd' ㄷ all sound like [t] at the end of a block, e.g., *kkoch* 꽃 -> [kkot]).

content.text: In Korean phonetics, the rule of {{c1::Liaison (Linkage)}} is defined as: A Batchim consonant followed by a word starting with a vowel carries over to the next syllable (e.g., *bap-eul* 밥을 -> [ba.beul]).
content.explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for Korean.
content.spanish: En la fonética de Korean, la regla de 'Liaison (Linkage)' es: A Batchim consonant followed by a word starting with a vowel carries over to the next syllable (e.g., *bap-eul* 밥을 -> [ba.beul]).
content.usage:
Rule: Liaison (Linkage)
Description: A Batchim consonant followed by a word starting with a vowel carries over to the next syllable (e.g., *bap-eul* 밥을 -> [ba.beul]).

content.text: In Korean phonetics, the rule of {{c1::Nasalization}} is defined as: Plain stop consonants (ㅂ, ㄷ, ㄱ) assimilate into nasals when followed by a nasal consonant (ㄴ, ㅁ) (e.g., *hap-ni-da* 합니다 -> [ham-ni-da], *kuk-mul* 국물 -> [kuŋ-mul]).
content.explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for Korean.
content.spanish: En la fonética de Korean, la regla de 'Nasalization' es: Plain stop consonants (ㅂ, ㄷ, ㄱ) assimilate into nasals when followed by a nasal consonant (ㄴ, ㅁ) (e.g., *hap-ni-da* 합니다 -> [ham-ni-da], *kuk-mul* 국물 -> [kuŋ-mul]).
content.usage:
Rule: Nasalization
Description: Plain stop consonants (ㅂ, ㄷ, ㄱ) assimilate into nasals when followed by a nasal consonant (ㄴ, ㅁ) (e.g., *hap-ni-da* 합니다 -> [ham-ni-da], *kuk-mul* 국물 -> [kuŋ-mul]).

content.text: In Korean phonetics, the rule of {{c1::Tensification}} is defined as: Plain consonants (ㅂ, ㄷ, ㄱ, ㅅ, ㅈ) become tense/fortis (ㅃ, ㄸ, ㄲ, ㅆ, ㅉ) when following a Batchim stop (e.g., *hak-kyo* 학교 -> [hak-kkyo]).
content.explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for Korean.
content.spanish: En la fonética de Korean, la regla de 'Tensification' es: Plain consonants (ㅂ, ㄷ, ㄱ, ㅅ, ㅈ) become tense/fortis (ㅃ, ㄸ, ㄲ, ㅆ, ㅉ) when following a Batchim stop (e.g., *hak-kyo* 학교 -> [hak-kkyo]).
content.usage:
Rule: Tensification
Description: Plain consonants (ㅂ, ㄷ, ㄱ, ㅅ, ㅈ) become tense/fortis (ㅃ, ㄸ, ㄲ, ㅆ, ㅉ) when following a Batchim stop (e.g., *hak-kyo* 학교 -> [hak-kkyo]).

content.text: In Korean phonetics, the rule of {{c1::Palatalization}} is defined as: Syllable final ㄷ (t) or ㅌ (tʰ) followed by 이 (i) is pronounced as [dʒ] (ㅈ) or [tʃ] (ㅊ) (e.g., *kat-i* 같이 -> [ga-tʃi] "gachi", *ha-tot-i* 해돋이 -> [hae-do-ji]).
content.explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for Korean.
content.spanish: En la fonética de Korean, la regla de 'Palatalization' es: Syllable final ㄷ (t) or ㅌ (tʰ) followed by 이 (i) is pronounced as [dʒ] (ㅈ) or [tʃ] (ㅊ) (e.g., *kat-i* 같이 -> [ga-tʃi] "gachi", *ha-tot-i* 해돋이 -> [hae-do-ji]).
content.usage:
Rule: Palatalization
Description: Syllable final ㄷ (t) or ㅌ (tʰ) followed by 이 (i) is pronounced as [dʒ] (ㅈ) or [tʃ] (ㅊ) (e.g., *kat-i* 같이 -> [ga-tʃi] "gachi", *ha-tot-i* 해돋이 -> [hae-do-ji]).

content.text: In Korean phonetics, the rule of {{c1::Liquid Assimilation}} is defined as: - When ㄴ (n) and ㄹ (l) meet, the ㄴ is pronounced as [l] (e.g., *shin-ra* 신라 -> [shilla]).
content.explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for Korean.
content.spanish: En la fonética de Korean, la regla de 'Liquid Assimilation' es: - When ㄴ (n) and ㄹ (l) meet, the ㄴ is pronounced as [l] (e.g., *shin-ra* 신라 -> [shilla]).
content.usage:
Rule: Liquid Assimilation
Description: - When ㄴ (n) and ㄹ (l) meet, the ㄴ is pronounced as [l] (e.g., *shin-ra* 신라 -> [shilla]).

content.text: In Korean phonetics, the rule of {{c1::i + eo}} is defined as: > **yeo** (e.g., *kaji-eo* -> "kajyeo" 가져).

Description: > **yeo** (e.g., *kaji-eo* -> "kajyeo" 가져).

content.text: In Korean phonetics, the rule of {{c1::ha + yeo}} is defined as: > **hae** (e.g., *ha-yeo* -> "hae" 해).
content.explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for Korean.
content.spanish: En la fonética de Korean, la regla de 'ha + yeo' es: > **hae** (e.g., *ha-yeo* -> "hae" 해).
content.usage:
Rule: ha + yeo
Description: > **hae** (e.g., *ha-yeo* -> "hae" 해).

content.text: In Korean phonetics, the rule of {{c1::o + a}} is defined as: > **wa** (e.g., *bo-a* -> "bwa" 봐).
content.explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for Korean.
content.spanish: En la fonética de Korean, la regla de 'o + a' es: > **wa** (e.g., *bo-a* -> "bwa" 봐).
content.usage:
Rule: o + a
Description: > **wa** (e.g., *bo-a* -> "bwa" 봐).

content.text: In Korean phonetics, the rule of {{c1::u + eo}} is defined as: > **weo** (e.g., *ju-eo* -> "jweo" 줘).
content.explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for Korean.
content.spanish: En la fonética de Korean, la regla de 'u + eo' es: > **weo** (e.g., *ju-eo* -> "jweo" 줘).
content.usage:
Rule: u + eo
Description: > **weo** (e.g., *ju-eo* -> "jweo" 줘).

content.text: In Korean phonetics, the rule of {{c1::Syllable-timed}} is defined as: Korean is generally considered syllable-timed.

content.text: In Korean phonetics, the rule of {{c1::Intonation}} is defined as: Pitch generally rises at the end of questions and remains relatively flat or falls in statements.
content.explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for Korean.
content.spanish: En la fonética de Korean, la regla de 'Intonation' es: Pitch generally rises at the end of questions and remains relatively flat or falls in statements.
content.usage:
Rule: Intonation
Description: Pitch generally rises at the end of questions and remains relatively flat or falls in statements.

content.text: In Korean phonetics, the rule of {{c1::Dialectal Variation}} is defined as: Some dialects (like Gyeongsang) maintain a system of pitch accent.
content.explanation: Phonetics and pronunciation guide for Korean.
content.spanish: En la fonética de Korean, la regla de 'Dialectal Variation' es: Some dialects (like Gyeongsang) maintain a system of pitch accent.

content.text: What is the Korean pronunciation shortcut/tip for {{c1::The "Hapnida" Rule}}? Never say "hap-ni-da" for 합니다. The 'ㅂ' must turn into an 'ㅁ' due to nasalization: say "ham-ni-da". This is the most common beginner error.
content.explanation: Pronunciation tip/shortcut for Korean.
content.spanish: ¿Cuál es el atajo/consejo de pronunciación de Korean para 'The "Hapnida" Rule'? Never say "hap-ni-da" for 합니다. The 'ㅂ' must turn into an 'ㅁ' due to nasalization: say "ham-ni-da". This is the most common beginner error.
content.usage:
Tip: The "Hapnida" Rule
Never say "hap-ni-da" for 합니다. The 'ㅂ' must turn into an 'ㅁ' due to nasalization: say "ham-ni-da". This is the most common beginner error.

content.text: What is the Korean pronunciation shortcut/tip for {{c1::The Palatalization Bridge}}? When 'ㅌ' (t) in 같이 (together) meets '이' (i), it naturally shifts to a 'ch' [tʃ] sound: "ga-chi". Do not try to pronounce it as "gat-ee".
content.explanation: Pronunciation tip/shortcut for Korean.
content.spanish: ¿Cuál es el atajo/consejo de pronunciación de Korean para 'The Palatalization Bridge'? When 'ㅌ' (t) in 같이 (together) meets '이' (i), it naturally shifts to a 'ch' [tʃ] sound: "ga-chi". Do not try to pronounce it as "gat-ee".
content.usage:
Tip: The Palatalization Bridge
When 'ㅌ' (t) in 같이 (together) meets '이' (i), it naturally shifts to a 'ch' [tʃ] sound: "ga-chi". Do not try to pronounce it as "gat-ee".
